title: UpQ Classification Rules
description: Rules used to classify a task into Now/Next/Later. Mirrors src/classification/rulesSchema.js exactly — if this drifts from that file, one of the two has a bug. Two shapes are accepted: the legacy two-boolean shape (no schemaVersion, or schemaVersion:1), and the schemaVersion:2 predicate tree. GET /auth/me/classification/export attaches $schema automatically; PUT /auth/me/classification and POST /auth/me/classification/validate both strip it before validating, so re-importing the exported file as-is works without editing it out.

legacyBucket.description: label is display-only. now uses overdue/priorities; next uses future_due/priorities; later conventionally has just a label.

not.description: Mirrors rulesSchema.js's RESERVED_V2_KEYS refine() check — a predicate node (field/op/all/any/not) here almost always means schemaVersion:2 was forgotten at the top level, not a genuinely legacy bucket with odd extra fields.

leafPredicate.description: A single condition. `value` is required for eq/includes/gt/lt/within_days and unused for overdue/future_due.

allNode.description: AND — every predicate in the list must match.

anyNode.description: OR — at least one predicate in the list must match.

emptyPredicate.description: Deliberately matches nothing — the conventional catch-all for `later`, which is never itself evaluated as a predicate (later = whatever falls through now/next).
